acceptance_checks: Real-key flow never asks for or displays raw API keys.
Every blocked account-aware panel shows a visible next action.
Diagnostic metadata can classify empty output without private payloads.
deliverables: Player-facing prerequisite status for key saved, scopes, sync job, snapshots, and selected workflow.
No-output empty states that name the missing lifecycle step.
Support bundle evidence names aligned with the trial defect triage classifications.
adopt_now.maturity_impact: Raises UX and support maturity from trial-ready to trial-observable.
adopt_now.reason: Recent user feedback says valid keys can connect without visible outputs. Aegis solves similar confusion with readiness cards and provider requirements.
adopt_now.title: Aegis-style credential readiness for real GW2 API key flow

acceptance_checks: No behavior regression in existing KB patch and achievement route gates.
New lifecycle helper is covered by unit tests and one smoke harness.
Generated artifacts remain deterministic and metadata-only.
deliverables: Shared status model for draft, reviewed, persisted, enabled, exported, archived, signed_off.
Common audit event renderer for KB, patch, route source, and release evidence objects.
One harness that validates lifecycle transitions for at least two existing domains.
adopt_now.maturity_impact: Improves delivery speed and reduces duplicate phase code.
adopt_now.reason: The current efficiency bottleneck is repeated horizontal implementation of the same operating lifecycle. Aegis uses shared pipeline and control-plane patterns.
adopt_now.title: Replace repeated review/persist/enable/export phase chains with lifecycle primitives

acceptance_checks: Invalid rule candidates default to disabled and cannot affect recommendations.
Findings cite source ids and evidence refs, not raw private payloads.
Validated rules expose dry-run impact counts before enable.
deliverables: Rule candidate validation against entity/action/relation schemas before promotion.
Conflict findings for unsupported relation, missing evidence, private/public mismatch, and stale patch source.
Markdown/CSV queue export for operator review.
adopt_now.maturity_impact: Raises KB-backed recommendation maturity from reviewed-rule MVP to auditable ontology operations.
adopt_now.reason: GW2Radar has strong schemas and layers, but Aegis has a clearer pattern for turning invalid semantic edges into reviewable work.
adopt_now.title: Aegis-style ontology conflict and missing evidence queue for GW2 rules

acceptance_checks: Must not replace current simple reviewed source files during trial.
deliverables: Post-trial operator workbench only if real reviewer throughput requires it.
defer.maturity_impact: Useful later for content operations, not needed for immediate player validation.
defer.reason: GW2Radar is in player-trial closeout; a full admin CMS would expand scope.
defer.title: Full Aegis-style domain pack CMS/admin

acceptance_checks: No dependency on external vector services for MVP player flows.
deliverables: Production data layer design after trial evidence confirms demand.
defer.maturity_impact: Production scalability improvement, not a current blocker.
defer.reason: Aegis uses deeper data platform pieces, but GW2Radar can validate product value locally first.
defer.title: Postgres pgvector GraphRAG parity

explicit_non_goals: Do not copy AegisRadar CO2 or market-entry business logic into GW2Radar.
Do not add automated trading, guaranteed profit claims, or gameplay automation.
Do not make GW2Radar depend on D:\Projects\AegisRadar at runtime.
Do not expose private account payloads, raw API keys, or unreviewed rule content in public artifacts.
next_priority: Implement p0_trial_key_flow_visibility first, then refactor repeated lifecycle code only where it directly shortens current KB, route, and release gate maintenance.

ontology_signals.gw2radar_mapping: GW2Radar already separates public_game, private_player_state, and personal_intelligence. The missing Aegis-style depth is explicit ontology validation and conflict surfacing for promoted rule packs.
ontology_signals.pattern: Evidence facts, intelligence graph, and ontology graph are separate layers.

ontology_signals.gw2radar_mapping: Route invalid GW2 rule links, unsupported patch claims, and private/public layer mismatches into review queues instead of silently excluding them.
ontology_signals.pattern: Invalid ontology relations and missing evidence become audit tasks.

ontology_signals.gw2radar_mapping: Extend existing reviewed KnowledgeRule enable gates with dry-run impact counts before enabling rules that affect player recommendations.
ontology_signals.pattern: Ontology rules can be dry-run, compared, viewed in history, and toggled.

process_flow_signals.gw2radar_mapping: Use reviewed GW2 KB/source packs and route source manifests as the equivalent configuration surface for planners, reports, and patch impact rules.
process_flow_signals.pattern: Domain Pack YAML drives extraction, scoring, audit rules, graph build, and exports.

process_flow_signals.gw2radar_mapping: Consolidate repeated GW2 review, promotion, export, archive, and sign-off flows behind shared lifecycle helpers instead of adding another bespoke phase chain.
process_flow_signals.pattern: A regular pipeline records reusable steps and can run offline demo paths.

process_flow_signals.gw2radar_mapping: Keep GW2 operator views separate from player views: support triage and release gates are operator surfaces, while account guidance remains player-facing.
process_flow_signals.pattern: Admin is an intelligence OS control plane with operable and read-only classes.

reference_repo_state: Read-only reference. The AegisRadar worktree has local changes, so GW2Radar must not copy files or depend on that repo at runtime.
summary: AegisRadar is most useful as a lifecycle and ontology operating model: domain-pack driven ingestion, separated fact/graph/ontology layers, audit-backed rule promotion, credential readiness, and a front-door UX organized around connect, choose, receive. GW2Radar should borrow those structures while keeping the GW2 player domain, privacy boundaries, and current trial stop-line.

ux_signals.gw2radar_mapping: Translate to GW2 player language: connect API key, choose intent (legendary, build, market, returner), receive next actions and report.
ux_signals.pattern: User journey is connect data sources, choose product, receive deliverable.

ux_signals.gw2radar_mapping: Use account permission readiness and sync health as visible prerequisites for account value, Build Fit, Legendary Planner, and Market Radar outputs.
ux_signals.pattern: Report readiness explains missing providers and disabled outputs.

ux_signals.gw2radar_mapping: Keep GW2 reports downloadable, but expose evidence lineage inline so senior players can verify why a recommendation was made.
ux_signals.pattern: Deliverables and lineage are separate destinations in the user journey.
